overview: The Village of Weston Community Life and Public Safety Committee met on May 4th to advance several ordinance changes and address community safety matters. Key actions included recommending approval of nuisance code amendments, an e-bike ordinance with emoto restrictions, mobile food vendor fire inspections, address corrections, and a raze order for an abandoned property at 4104 Shore Avenue.
committee: Community Life and Public Safety Committee

agenda.item: Call to order and Pledge of Allegiance
Approval of minutes from previous meeting
Mobile food vendor fire inspection ordinance

discussions.body: Jim Senalt provided extensive public comment criticizing the new farmers market location at Veteran's Memorial Park, expressing concerns that grass parking will destroy the park and that ADA accessible spaces placed on grass violate requirements for firm surfaces. He questioned the origin of new fire inspections for food trucks and noted that several code enforcement cases have been pending for years without visible progress.

Roman and Audrey presented the code enforcement report. Staff noted that cases with enforcement suspended may move back to pending status now that construction season has begun. Good news was reported that 3020 Mountain View Avenue will be removed from the code enforcement roll after the property sale was completed and the new owner submitted permits for repairs.

Chief Hunt reported 2,394 calls for March (77 per day, 3.2 per hour), 410 traffic stops, and 281 citations issued. The department has hired its 40th officer, with the 38th starting last week, 39th starting in May, and 40th around January. Two SROs work with zoning violations during summer for all three municipalities, also handling pet licensing compliance.

Committee discussed final copy of proposed nuisance code amendments, including the tree limb height requirements (14 feet over streets, 10 feet over walkways) that were switched as discussed previously. Motion to recommend approval to board of trustees made by Olsen, seconded by Kimi. Passed unanimously.

Extensive discussion on e-bike regulations. Chief Hunt strongly recommended prohibiting emotos (electric dirt bikes) from roadways, sidewalks, and trails due to safety concerns, comparing them to unlicensed dirt bikes. Committee debated whether to also restrict electric scooters but decided to leave officer discretion for children's play scooters. Motion by Olsen to recommend approval with addition that emotos are prohibited from operating on roadways, sidewalks, trails, or any public property in the Village of Weston. Seconded by Chwanka. Passed unanimously.

Brent Tolson presented research showing Weston's 12-hour overnight parking window (4am-4pm, October 31-May 1) is far outside other municipalities which typically have 4-5 hour windows. Michael (Public Works) suggested either eliminating the regulation entirely and signing specific problem areas, or matching Rothschild's regulations. Chief Hunt preferred keeping overnight restrictions to give officers time to clear cars before plowing. Committee decided to table the item until July, directing Michael to identify specific problem areas and develop logistics for a more targeted approach.

Mount Olive Church requested a crosswalk between their property and DC Everest administrative parking lot. Michael estimated a basic crosswalk would cost approximately $2,000-$3,000, while a lighted system would cost $25,000-$30,000. Committee members expressed reluctance to pay for crosswalks requested by private entities. Motion by Olsen that if Mount Olive is willing to cover the cost of installation, committee recommends board approval. Seconded by Chwanka. Passed unanimously. Michael to contact Mount Olive regarding cost-sharing.

Chief presented ordinance 26-8 creating a regional fire inspection program for food trucks involving Wisconsin Rapids, Marshfield, Stevens Point, Plover, Rib Mountain, and Safer. Inspections would be valid across all participating municipalities with a sticker system. Wausau opted not to participate, preferring their own system. Chief noted one recent inspection failed due to galvanized steel LP piping and improperly secured propane containers. Motion by Chwanka to approve ordinance 26-8, seconded by Campkey. Passed unanimously.

Staff presented three address correction requests for E911 compliance: 5915 Delicoski Street (duplex needing separate addresses), properties on Ross Avenue that are out of sequence, and 2211 Radkey Avenue for a new duplex on a private drive. Chief Hunt added another address issue at Weston Villa Apartments on Delicoski where an 8-plex has nine separate addresses. Motion to recommend approval passed unanimously.

Staff requested authorization to begin raze proceedings on an abandoned structure at 4104 Shore Avenue. The property has been abandoned since 2010-2011 and was too deteriorated for a fire training burn. Previous owners had housed chickens inside the structure. Current owners are up to date on taxes and preferred the raze approach. Costs will be added to the tax roll. Motion by Olsen, seconded by Campkey. Passed unanimously.

publicComment: Jim Senalt spoke during public comment, criticizing the new farmers market location at Veteran's Memorial Park. He raised concerns about vehicles parking on grass potentially violating village code, ADA accessible spaces being placed on grass rather than asphalt, the origin of new fire inspection requirements for food trucks, and the lack of progress on long-standing code enforcement cases. He suggested the committee should receive regular updates on older enforcement cases.
actionItems: Recommend approval of Chapter 50 nuisance amendments to board of trustees
Recommend approval of e-bike ordinance with emoto prohibition on roadways, sidewalks, trails, and public property to board of trustees
Michael (Public Works) to develop list of specific areas needing special parking signage for overnight parking discussion in July
Michael to contact Mount Olive Church regarding willingness to pay for crosswalk installation on Alderson Street
Recommend approval of ordinance 26-8 for mobile food vendor fire inspections to board of trustees
Recommend approval of address corrections for E911 compliance to board of trustees
Authorize staff to begin raze order proceedings on 4104 Shore Avenue
Staff to investigate property condition issue at Sternberg and South Timber
Next meeting scheduled for Monday, July 7th at 5:00 PM
title: Community Life and Public Safety
